seo.title: The Heights vs SoMa District: Little Rock Neighborhood Comparison | Metro Little Rock
seo.description: Comparing The Heights and SoMa (South Main) in Little Rock — home prices, restaurants, walkability, vibe, and which neighborhood fits your lifestyle.

intro: Both are Little Rock's most buzzed-about urban neighborhoods — but they attract completely different buyers. The Heights is established, expensive, and food-forward. SoMa is emerging, more affordable, and arts-forward. Here's the honest comparison.

metrics.notes: SoMa offers significantly more value per dollar for buyers willing to embrace a neighborhood still in transition.
The Heights has a true walkable corridor with restaurants, coffee, pharmacy, and Saturday farmers market.

metrics.b_value: Good — South on Main anchors it
metrics.notes: The Heights has more total options; SoMa has South on Main, which is the best restaurant in either neighborhood.
Lost Forty Brewing and the SoMa bar scene give it an edge for younger buyers who prioritize nightlife.
SoMa is Little Rock's arts district. The Heights has character but not an arts scene.
Both have distinctive architectural character — different eras and styles, equally appealing.
The Heights is fully stabilized. SoMa is actively gentrifying — safer than 5 years ago but still patchy.
The Heights has a clear school advantage with Pulaski Heights Elementary being one of LRSD's best.
SoMa buyers are likely still in the early-to-mid phases of a long gentrification curve.

summary: Choose The Heights if you want an established, walkable neighborhood with proven restaurant access, strong elementary schools, and don't mind paying a premium for stability. Choose SoMa if you want more house for your money, a genuine arts and brewery culture, and are comfortable with a neighborhood still in active transition. Both are great urban neighborhoods — they serve different buyer profiles.
